claims.subject: Lazada Singapore
claims.evidence: lazada sg midmarket, that was pricing. their contract came up in february and a local competitor came in with a number we couldn't match for that volume tier.
claims.object_value: pricing — local competitor undercut HelixPay on volume tier

claims.subject: Tom Holloway
claims.evidence: we run quarterly business reviews with our top twenty sea accounts. that's the cadence — top twenty by arr, refreshed each quarter.
claims.object_value: top 20 SEA accounts by ARR, refreshed each quarter

claims.subject: Tom Holloway
claims.evidence: about three hours per account on average.
claims.object_value: about three hours per account on average

claims.subject: Tom Holloway
claims.evidence: i pull usage data from the product warehouse, ticket history from zendesk, csm notes from gainsight, deal context and renewal date from pipedrive — well, hubspot now, for the accounts that migrated, pipedrive for the ones that didn't yet. and then nps verbatims if they've responded to a survey recently. five tools to build one brief.
claims.object_value: product warehouse, Zendesk, Gainsight, Pipedrive (or HubSpot for migrated accounts), NPS survey tool — five tools

claims.subject: Tom Holloway
claims.evidence: finance — i sometimes need to check invoice payment status because late payment is a churn signal. that's another tool. and slack search if i remember a conversation about the customer happened. so call it six.
claims.object_value: six tools total (product warehouse, Zendesk, Gainsight, Pipedrive/HubSpot, NPS survey tool, finance/invoice tool, Slack)

claims.subject: CRM migration
claims.evidence: hubspot now, for the accounts that migrated, pipedrive for the ones that didn't yet
claims.object_value: partial — some accounts migrated to HubSpot, others still on Pipedrive

claims.subject: Tom Holloway
claims.evidence: three signals i look at, in this order. login frequency — if a merchant's daily active sessions on the dashboard drop noticeably for two weeks running, something's off. ticket volume — but you have to read it carefully... and nps and qbr verbatims, the qualitative stuff.
claims.object_value: 1) login frequency (daily active sessions), 2) ticket volume (with qualitative review), 3) NPS and QBR verbatims

claims.subject: unnamed customer (Q4 QBR)
claims.evidence: their qbr in q4 was tense. their cfo basically said "we're going to give you one more cycle." i flagged it red on the renewals tracker
claims.object_value: flagged red on renewals tracker after Q4 QBR; CFO gave one-more-cycle warning
